seoTitle: How to Integrate ClickUp with Microsoft Teams
metaDescription: Learn how to install ClickUp inside Microsoft Teams, embed live task lists into channel tabs, and turn chat messages into tasks.
intro: If your team communicates in Microsoft Teams but tracks work in ClickUp, this integration cuts down on switching between apps. This guide covers installing the official ClickUp extension from the Teams App Store, embedding a live ClickUp List into a channel tab, and using message shortcuts to turn a chat thread directly into a task.

steps.heading: Open the Teams App Store
steps.body: In Microsoft Teams, click the Apps icon in the bottom left navigation sidebar to browse available integrations.

steps.heading: Install the ClickUp extension
steps.body: Search for ClickUp, select the official extension, and click Add to install it.

steps.heading: Authorize the connection
steps.body: Pass the OAuth handshake gateway and select your target ClickUp workspace to link the two platforms.

steps.heading: Add a ClickUp tab to a channel
steps.body: Inside a specific Teams channel, use the top bar to add a new tab and select ClickUp, then choose your target Space, Folder, or List.

steps.heading: Set up notification triggers
steps.body: Configure the channel notification framework so specific task status changes automatically post as chat alerts.

steps.heading: Turn chat messages into tasks
steps.body: Hover over a chat bubble and use the message shortcut to instantly generate a new ClickUp task from that conversation.

faq.question: Can I see my ClickUp tasks directly inside a Teams channel?
faq.answer: Yes, embedding a ClickUp List, Whiteboard, or Doc into a channel tab lets your team view live task data without leaving Teams.

faq.question: Does this integration notify the channel when a task changes?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure notification rules so status, assignee, or priority changes in ClickUp automatically post as alerts in the linked channel.

faq.question: Can I create a task from an existing chat message?
faq.answer: Yes, hovering over a message reveals a shortcut to instantly convert that chat thread into a new ClickUp task.

faq.question: Do I need admin permission to install ClickUp in Teams?
faq.answer: That depends on your organization's Teams app policies, since some admins restrict which third-party apps members can install.
